title: Cochliopetalum albiflos
taxonomy: Cochliopetalum albiflos is a species of flowering plant. It is classified as a member of the family Annonaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the custard-apple or soursop family. The Annonaceae family consists of around 130 genera and over 2,200 species. Cochliopetalum albiflos is native to Mexico.

intro: Cochliopetalum albiflos, also known as the White-flowered Cochliopetalum, is a perennial plant native to Mexico, belonging to the family Annonaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering various health benefits, including its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cochliopetalum albiflos include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory conditions.
